title: findRepoRoot linked-worktree hop + BEE_ROOT override + resolveRoots (lib, both mirrors) — RED-first temp-worktree tests
action: Per D2 (decisions 9ba51eb0/5aa8946d): in lib/state.mjs (both mirror copies), extend findRepoRoot: (1) FIRST check process.env.BEE_ROOT — valid only when <BEE_ROOT>/.bee/onboarding.json exists, else fall through silently; (2) after the existing walk resolves a root, add the linked-worktree hop — when <root>/.git is a FILE, parse its 'gitdir: <path>' line (absolute or relative; tolerate trailing whitespace and win32 backslashes); when that gitdir path matches <main>/.git/worktrees/<id>, derive <main> and return it ONLY when <main>/.bee/onboarding.json exists; any parse failure or missing onboarding = keep the walk's root (fail-open to today's behavior). Never loop (one hop max). Add export resolveRoots(startDir) -> {storeRoot, workRoot}: workRoot = the walk's root (worktree top when in one), storeRoot = after BEE_ROOT/hop resolution; equal outside worktrees. RED-first rows in test_lib.mjs: build a real temp git repo (git init, commit, git worktree add), assert findRepoRoot from inside the worktree returns the MAIN root; from the main root unchanged; BEE_ROOT valid/invalid; corrupt .git file falls back to worktree root; resolveRoots pair correct in and out of worktrees; gitdir with trailing newline; relative gitdir.

truths: outside any worktree, findRepoRoot behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged (regression rows prove it)
inside a linked worktree, storeRoot = main checkout iff main carries .bee/onboarding.json; otherwise today's behavior
BEE_ROOT pointing at a non-bee directory is ignored (falls through), never trusted
the hop never recurses and never escapes above the gitdir-named main root
artifacts: hop + BEE_ROOT + resolveRoots in both state.mjs copies
prohibitions: changing the walk order for non-worktree cases
any network/exec call in root resolution (fs reads only)
